response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with impressive breadth across web, mobile, and high-performance systems, but he does not meet the role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments over a decade ago, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He shows no SQL Server or Oracle experience (his database work is MongoDB and MySQL), and he has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure — his closest touchpoint, RIDE Capital, was a TypeScript/Node fintech role in investment administration rather than capital markets systems.
strengths: Deep low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling
Broad polyglot engineering background with some C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components)
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ work, ending in 2015 — far below the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB and MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
